briefing.headline: Cover water — pre-spawn stained conditions reward moving baits that trigger reaction strikes.
briefing.gameplan:
In pre-spawn with stained water and stable conditions, moving baits presentations shine.

Wheeler: Keep paddle tail swimbaits near the bottom — fishing too far off bottom is the biggest mistake; use lighter line (12-lb fluoro) and heavier head to achieve deeper running depth and better action

Key adjustment for stable: moderate cadence — match the natural pace of local forage.

primaryApproach.retrieve: Wheeler: Keep paddle tail swimbaits near the bottom — fishing too far off bottom is the biggest mistake; use lighter line (12-lb fluoro) and heavier head to achieve deeper running depth and better act
primaryApproach.targets: Focus on grass, flat, dock. Work the transitions and edges where structure changes.

alternateApproach.retrieve: KVD: Thunder Cricket in shallow grass even in upper 30s water. Use 3/8 oz (lighter than normal) with straight-tail blade minnow trailer for subtle action. Pull and stop, not steady retrieve. Get it do
alternateApproach.targets: Focus on grass, flat, dock. Work the transitions and edges where structure changes.

proInsights.insight: KVD: 'Three swimbait styles for different conditions: Bulkier hollow-body for slower presentations — great prespawn and stained water for extra vibration. Subtle finesse swimbaits (like Swimming Shiner) for clear water and tough bites on bright da...

Card (pro): 'Single Tail Grub works all year but great during pre-spawn for smallmouth. Fish it like a small swimbait, reeling slow enough to just tick rocks. Also reel-reel-reel then let fall on tight line'

Palaniuk: 'The spot had a riprap bank with a steep drop, then a trough that came up onto a hard bottom ridge, tapered back off into another trough. The trough made a horseshoe. A 40-yard section where the fish wanted to live, with a 25-foot hole t...

briefing.depthStrategy: Stable pre-spawn conditions mean fish are staging predictably at 5-12ft on points, channel swings, and riprap banks. They're feeding aggressively as water warms. Work the transition zone between deep winter haunts and shallow spawning areas.
adjustIf: If conditions suddenly shift (pressure drop, wind change), be ready to speed up your approach — transitional moments produce big bites
If water clears significantly, downsize presentations and move to natural color patterns
If moving baits aren't getting bit, slow down to a crawling retrieve or switch to a jig
